rules.reason: Proxy password placeholder example
Proxy password templated variable reference
Allowlist inline detect-secrets
js-tokens is a well-known npm package, not a secret
Ansible templated variable reference, not a hardcoded secret
NETBOX_TOKEN is a placeholder/dummy token used in functional tests Dockerfile
Function parameter default value, not a hardcoded secret
Ansible Jinja2 templated proxy username reference, not a hardcoded secret
Nil UUID placeholder used as example in documentation, not a real secret
Variable assigned from HVAC client API call, not a hardcoded secret
Conditional check on API response dict, not a hardcoded secret
Variable assigned from dict access on API response, not a hardcoded secret
CI_JOB_TOKEN read from environment variable, not hardcoded
JOB-TOKEN header uses CI variable reference, not a hardcoded secret
package-lock.json is auto-generated; integrity hashes and npm package names are not secrets
poetry.lock is auto-generated; package wheel hashes (sha256) and dependency specs are not secrets
PASSWORD: '$PASSWORD' is a shell variable reference in CI, not a hardcoded secret
PASSWORD read from environment variable with error sentinel default, not a hardcoded secret
password unpacked from Base64-decoded credentials, not a hardcoded secret
DB_PWD placeholder value using angle-bracket template syntax, not a real password
db_pwd read from environment variable, not a hardcoded secret
Password assigned from kwargs dict (runtime argument), not a hardcoded secret
password read from environment variable, not a hardcoded secret
Ansible Jinja2 templated secret reference, not a hardcoded secret
Ansible password lookup from environment variable, not a hardcoded secret
Ansible Jinja2 variable reference to vmware credentials, not a hardcoded secret
Ansible Jinja2 variable reference for SNMP auth password, not a hardcoded secret
Ansible Jinja2 variable reference for update_password, not a hardcoded secret
Ansible adminpwd lookup from environment variable, not a hardcoded secret
Ansible password lookup from environment variable (vcenter), not a hardcoded secret
Generic Ansible/Jinja2 templated credential reference in YAML — variable, not a hardcoded value
Password/secret/token assigned null, None, or empty string in YAML — not a real credential
Any YAML key containing token/secret/password set to empty string — placeholder, not a real credential
Password/secret/token assigned an angle-bracket template placeholder, not a real credential
Shell or environment variable reference assigned to password/secret/token — not a hardcoded secret
Shell export of TOKEN/SECRET/PASSWORD from an environment variable — not a hardcoded secret
Token/secret/apikey read from Django settings attribute or decouple config() — not hardcoded
CPE (Common Platform Enumeration) identifier in SBOM/dependency scan output — not a secret
SPDX packageVerificationCodeValue is a content hash for SBOM integrity verification — not a secret
Hashicorp Vault secret path reference in documentation/comments — not a hardcoded secret
rules.linePattern: (?i)key of the following secret in Hashicorp Vault

rules.reason: sshpass uses Ansible Jinja2 variable reference for password — not a hardcoded credential
Ansible lookup 'secret' parameter references Vault path via Jinja2 template — not a hardcoded secret
Docker volume mount using shell variable ${pwd} or $pwd — not a secret
enc_token_url constructed from provider host variable — not a hardcoded secret
X-Bluecoat-Enc-Token HTTP header assigned from variable enc_token.rstrip() — not a hardcoded secret
vault_namespace/vault_path keys assigned from Jinja2 filter expression — not a hardcoded secret
private_key_pwd key assigned from Jinja2 variable reference — not a hardcoded secret
Ansible lookup from Vault approle with .secret.passphrase — runtime secret retrieval, not hardcoded
Ansible lookup('env', ...) for Docker registry password — environment variable reference, not hardcoded
Dockerfile ENV token/password set to empty string — not a real credential
Shell export of VAULT_TOKEN from command substitution $(...) — not a hardcoded secret
vault write auth/approle/login uses env-var role_id/secret_id — not hardcoded credentials
Python string literal containing a secret-keyword regex pattern — part of detection tooling, not a real secret
Auto-generated API client Python class with Token/Secret in name — not a hardcoded secret
Auto-generated API client instantiation via cls() classmethod — not a hardcoded secret
Auto-generated API client .additional_properties = d pattern — not a hardcoded secret
token passed as keyword argument from credentials object — runtime value, not hardcoded
token function/method parameter with None or str default — not a hardcoded secret
API client __init__ passing token variable as keyword argument — not a hardcoded secret
Dataclass field reading token from environment via _default_env helper — not a hardcoded secret
Auto-generated OpenAPI/Netbox client: secret_* typed as Union[Unset, ...] = UNSET — filter parameter, not a credential
Auto-generated OpenAPI/Netbox client: params dict assignment of secret_* filter — not a credential
Auto-generated OpenAPI/Netbox client: json_secret_* = secret_* local variable serialization — not a credential
Auto-generated OpenAPI/Netbox client: isinstance Unset guard on secret_* — not a credential
Auto-generated OpenAPI/Netbox client: function parameter named secret_* with Union[Unset, ...] type — filter arg, not a credential
Ansible VAR_NETBOX_TOKEN assigned from Jinja2 variable or Vault result — not a hardcoded secret
Ansible Netbox nb_lookup query with token=VAR_NETBOX_TOKEN — runtime variable, not a hardcoded secret
Ansible become password from Vault result dict — runtime value, not a hardcoded secret
Ansible bare variable name VAR_NETBOX_TOKEN used as YAML value — variable reference, not a hardcoded token
CI shell env assignment of API_KEY/API_TOKEN from shell variable — not a hardcoded secret
Robot Framework -v API_TOKEN: CLI argument with shell variable reference — not a hardcoded secret
Ansible lookup('env', '...') with optional | default() filter — environment variable reference, not a hardcoded secret
ALLCAPS Ansible variable key assigned from Jinja2 lookup('env', ...) — environment variable reference, not a hardcoded secret
awx_token assigned from AWX API JSON response — runtime token, not a hardcoded secret
controller_password assigned from Ansible lookup('env', ...) — environment variable reference, not a hardcoded secret
PRIVATE-TOKEN HTTP header (hyphenated key) assigned from Jinja2 variable — not a hardcoded secret
secret_value assigned null or empty YAML block scalar (>-) — placeholder variable, not a real secret
secret_exists boolean check on secret_value variable — conditional expression, not a hardcoded secret
Ansible debug msg referencing secret_exists boolean variable — not a hardcoded secret
community.aws.secretsmanager_secret is an Ansible module name — not a hardcoded secret
secret key assigned from Jinja2 privatekey/publickey/passphrase result variable — runtime value, not a hardcoded secret
Python SECRET_KEY read from environment variable via os.environ.get — not a hardcoded secret
Python guard: if os.environ.get('...SECRET...') — conditional env var check, not a hardcoded secret
Python SECRET_KEY read from file on disk (e.g. /etc/tower/SECRET_KEY) — not a hardcoded secret
Python check for existence of a secret key file on disk — not a hardcoded secret
Python SECRET_KEY generated from cryptographically random bytes (os.urandom) — not a hardcoded secret
Shell/env variable reference for DJANGO_SUPERUSER_PASSWORD, AWX_SECRET_KEY, POSTGRES_PASSWORD — not hardcoded
Any YAML key containing password/secret/token/key assigned from Jinja2 expression — not a hardcoded secret
Ansible hostvars reference for netbox_token or similar — runtime inventory value, not a hardcoded secret
Python secret_key = lookup('env', ...) assignment (no Jinja2) — environment variable reference, not a hardcoded secret
Python token/id_token/refresh_token extracted from API JSON response dict — runtime value, not a hardcoded secret
OAuth 2.0 standard URN token type string — protocol identifier, not a secret
refresh_token_max_age is a numeric TTL configuration value — not a secret
EULOGIN_*_SECRET env var set to empty string or shell variable reference — not a real secret
js-tokens package reference in any lock or dependency file (yarn.lock, pip, etc.) — npm/pip package name, not a secret
Squid proxy error page template: username/password error message using $(exception.last_error) — not a hardcoded secret
rules.linePattern: (?i)while preparing to send your username.password upstream

rules.reason: HTML password form element (div/form/input/label) — UI markup, not a hardcoded secret
HTML file or Squid error template — static markup files never contain real secrets
Ansible 'secret: true' is a task-level boolean flag to hide output from logs — not a hardcoded secret value
Proxy URL with angle-bracket placeholder credentials in shell export — documentation/example, not a real secret
git config --global *.proxy with angle-bracket placeholder credentials — documentation example, not a real secret
proxy ini/config setting with angle-bracket placeholder credentials — documentation example, not a real secret
Documentation sentence describing proxy password usage — prose text, not a hardcoded secret
Excel #NOM? or #NAME? formula error value in CSV/spreadsheet export — not a secret
Node-RED / JS function: username/password read from msg.response or msg.authorization — runtime message properties, not hardcoded
Node-RED / JS function: URL constructed from $env() and msg.authorization credentials — runtime values, not hardcoded
Node-RED / JS function: Authorization header read from msg.req.headers — runtime HTTP header, not a hardcoded secret
Node-RED commented-out credentialSecret placeholder — comment line with example value, not a real secret
PostgreSQL dump comment '-- Name: *token*' identifies a sequence/table object — metadata, not a hardcoded secret
PostgreSQL dump files are schema/data exports — all findings in .sql/.dump files are auto-generated metadata
Bare proxy URL with angle-bracket placeholder credentials — documentation example, not a real secret
JSON key 'password' with angle-bracket placeholder value — documentation/example JSON, not a real credential
Documentation note about token expiry/reconfiguration — prose text, not a hardcoded secret
rules.linePattern: (?i)the token configure is no more valid

rules.reason: JSON/YAML 'password' key mapped to an object {} — nested structure, not a credential string
Type annotation 'password : String' in GraphQL schema or similar — type declaration, not a credential
sudoers NOPASSWD rule in Ansible YAML line key — sudo configuration, not a credential
Shell env var with secret/password/token name assigned empty value (VAR=) — unset variable, not a credential
CI_JOB_TOKEN_WRITE assigned from shell variable reference ${CI_TOKEN_WRITE} — not a hardcoded secret
Python secret = vault.get_keys_data(...) or vault.read_secret(...) — Vault API call with variable args, not a hardcoded secret
Python with Vault(...secret_id=VARIABLE...) context manager — all args are variable references, not hardcoded secrets
Python SECRET_*/VAULT_* assigned via getenv() — environment variable reference, not a hardcoded secret
Docstring/comment describing get_password_by_app_role function — documentation text, not a hardcoded secret
HVAC client token set from Vault API response dict — runtime value, not a hardcoded secret
Python method signature with secret/password/token in name — function definition, not a hardcoded secret
Python 'if secret is not None:' guard — conditional check on variable, not a hardcoded secret
Python secret_data/secret initialized to False/None/empty — sentinel value, not a hardcoded secret
detect-secrets baseline fingerprint entry (sha1:file:type:line) — scan metadata, not a hardcoded secret
Markdown link to create a personal access token in user settings — documentation text, not a hardcoded secret
OpenAPI/Swagger security scheme auth requirement — '- tokenAuth: []' declares an auth scheme, not a token value
Git commit log entry flagged as sourcegraph token — short commit hash + author/date metadata, not a secret
Ansible AWX controller_password/url_password set from AWX_PASSWORD env var with default() — env var reference, not a hardcoded secret
Python token=netbox_token keyword argument — passing a variable, not a hardcoded secret
API_KEY set from os.getenv() — env var read, not a hardcoded key
Netbox token set from env var lookup or Jinja2 variable — Ansible reference, not a hardcoded token
GraphQL nextToken field typed as String — schema type declaration, not a token value
AWS Smithy/AppSync @paginated decorator with inputToken/outputToken — pagination metadata, not a secret
SUPERUSER_API_TOKEN/REDIS_PASSWORD/EMAIL_PASSWORD/DATABASE_PASSWORD set to test sentinel or empty — well-known dev/test placeholder value
Django example/demo SECRET_KEY containing alphabet sequence — clearly a placeholder, not a real key
POSTGRES_PASSWORD/REDIS_PASSWORD set to 'netbox' or 'password' — well-known default dev value for netbox stack
Proxy URL with literal angle-bracket placeholder values — documentation template, not a real credential
Markdown section heading mentioning 'Secrets management' — not a secret value
password set to angle-bracket placeholder '<...>' — template or documentation value, not a real credential
Ansible vault_result set from community.hashi_vault lookup — Vault lookup expression, not a hardcoded secret
"PASSWORD": "" or "PASSWORD": "netbox" — empty or well-known netbox default in JSON config
PostgreSQL dump metadata line '-- Name: users_token ...' — schema DDL comment, not a secret
ENV NETBOX_TOKEN="" — Docker ENV instruction with empty token value
token=settings.NETBOX_TOKEN or similar — passing a Django/settings attribute, not a hardcoded token
Robot Framework / pytest CLI invocation with env var substitution — CI command, not a hardcoded secret
secret_id={{ VAULT_SECRET_ID }} — non-Jinja2 template style Vault variable substitution, not a hardcoded secret
Smithy/AppSync pagination outputToken/pageSize annotation — API schema metadata, not a secret
Markdown documentation mentioning proxy password in angle brackets — template documentation, not a real credential
Documentation prose about proxy authentication errors — instructional text, not a secret
Markdown example '* password: password' — documentation example showing field name, not a real password
Markdown documentation 'Via the API with an user token' — instructional prose, not a secret
Commented-out Node-RED example bcrypt hash — default template config line starting with //, not an active credential
X-API-KEY header set to Jinja2 variable {{ token }} — template variable reference, not a hardcoded key
secret_key=bubble.provider_credentials.password — attribute access chain passing a variable, not a hardcoded secret
secret: true — Ansible/YAML boolean flag to mask task output in logs, not a hardcoded secret value
access_token set from Jinja2 API response variable — dynamic value from prior HTTP call, not a hardcoded token
role_id=self.role_id, secret_id=self.secret_id — Python self attribute kwargs, passing instance variables not hardcoded values
Any YAML field containing 'password' or 'secret_key' whose value is purely a Jinja2 {{ expression }} — template variable, never a hardcoded secret
Python secret_key=lookup('env', ...) — env lookup call, not a hardcoded value
http_proxy/https_proxy set to a fully Jinja2-templated URL — proxy config template, not a real credential
REDIS_PASSWORD=only4testingpurpose — well-known test sentinel value, not a real password
netbox_token set from API response JSON variable — dynamic value from prior API call, not hardcoded
Python netbox_token argspec definition — module argument spec declaring a str parameter, not a hardcoded token
Python netbox_token = module.params.get(...) — reading a module parameter, not a hardcoded token
